label: Ideation Stage
productWhatToConsider:
Align product/service concept with a real social/environmental and financial need.
Think about materials, waste, energy needed to build and distribute.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Define service values that contribute to ethical or social impact.
Consider digital tools, human resource requirements, time efficiency.

label: Validation Stage (Problem/Solution Fit)
productWhatToConsider:
Validate that your product/service improves lives without causing harm.
Ensure solution can serve underserved or broad user groups.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Validate that your service benefits users equitably and ethically.
Design service to be accessible and inclusive for different communities.

workbookKey: 03_Prototype / Minimum Viable Product Development (Product/Market Fit Begins)
label: Prototype / Minimum Viable Product Development
productWhatToConsider:
Use sustainable strategies/materials, modular design, low-impact prototyping.
Avoid overproduction; use recyclable components.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Minimize overuse of platforms/tools, optimize human involvement.
Limit redundancy, avoid unnecessary complexity in service delivery.

label: Pre-Launch / Market Entry Preparation
productWhatToConsider:
Source responsibly (e.g., fair labor, low emissions suppliers).
Instill sustainability values in team decisions.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Choose ethical digital infrastructure (hosting, APIs, etc.).
Train service team in sustainable practices and inclusive service.

label: Launch / Early Commercial Activity
productWhatToConsider:
Minimize shipping, packaging, energy in delivery.
Market product sustainability honestly.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Reduce travel, optimize virtual or hybrid service models.
Be transparent about the service’s limitations and values.

label: Product-Market Fit
productWhatToConsider:
Educate users on sustainable use, maintenance, and disposal.
Use customer feedback to improve environmental/social impact.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Train users on best practices for efficiency, ethical use.
Get feedback on inclusivity, accessibility, and value of the service.

label: Growth & Channel Fit
productWhatToConsider:
Ensure supply chain, production, and logistics scale responsibly.
Monitor and reduce carbon footprint of production and delivery.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Ensure team and systems scale without harming people or overusing resources.
Use carbon-efficient tools, promote remote service where possible.

label: Revenue Validation / Business Model Fit
productWhatToConsider:
Explore reuse, recycling, or refurbishing and other potential uses
Achieve margins without exploiting resources or people.
serviceWhatToConsider:
Offer sustainable service models (e.g., shared economy, pay-as-you-go).
Ensure pricing reflects ethical labor and long-term viability.

label: Operational Foundation
productWhatToConsider: Create policies on sustainability, diversity, and ethics.
serviceWhatToConsider: Embed sustainability in service manuals, onboarding, and policies.

label: Early Scale / Fundraising Readiness
productWhatToConsider: Set up ESG reporting if raising funds or entering regulated markets.
serviceWhatToConsider: Establish governance for service quality, ethics, and impact.
